probes.name: age-group-tables-migrated
probes.description: The `age_groups` table exists + carries the latest schema version. Failure indicates a missed migration.

probes.name: age-group-default-catalog-loaded
probes.description: `data/default-age-groups.json` parses cleanly + contains the expected 10-entry baseline (U6, U8, U10, U12, U14, U16, U18, Adult, Senior, Masters). Failure indicates a corrupted deploy artifact.

probes.name: age-group-tenant-fk-integrity
probes.description: Every `age_groups.tenant_id` resolves to a live `tenants` row. FK is CASCADE so the DB should never permit orphans; this probe catches direct SQL manipulation.

probes.name: age-group-organization-fk-integrity
probes.description: Every non-null `age_groups.organization_id` resolves to a live `organizations` row AND belongs to the same tenant. Catches cross-tenant FK smuggling attempts + orphaned rows after direct SQL manipulation.

probes.name: age-group-default-invariant
probes.description: Every non-empty (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple has EXACTLY ONE row with `is_default = true`. Failure is a data-quality signal; the observer normally prevents this state, but the probe catches drift from bulk-imports + direct SQL.

probes.name: age-group-seeded-catalog-invariant
probes.description: Every tenant with at least one User has the seeded 10-entry catalog (is_seeded=true). Missing seed rows indicate a legacy tenant that provisioned before the age-group module landed — fix via `age-group:seed-defaults-for-tenant --all`.

probes.name: age-group-slug-uniqueness
probes.description: No two active AgeGroups share (tenant_id, organization_id, sport_key, slug). Backed by the partial unique index; the probe verifies the index is in place + healthy.

probes.name: age-group-bounds-consistency
probes.description: Every AgeGroup satisfies min_age_inclusive <= max_age_inclusive AND is_youth = (max_age_inclusive < 18). Catches drift from raw SQL edits that bypass the observer.

probes.name: age-group-cutoff-consistency
probes.description: Every AgeGroup has a cutoff_date_kind + cutoff_month + cutoff_day triple that satisfies the kind-specific constraints (see `rules.json` valid_cutoff_date).

probes.name: age-group-purge-queue-drained
probes.description: No `PurgeArchivedAgeGroupsJob` has been pending for > 48h. Catches queue stalls that could push an archived AgeGroup past its 1095-day retention window.
